commit.message: chore: Update README to mark the completion of the Personal Profile feature, reflecting its readiness for use in AI responses.
chore: Update README to reflect the new release version 0.7.0, ensuring accurate versioning in project documentation.

chore: Update marketing version to 0.7.0 in project configuration files to reflect the latest release. This change ensures consistency across all targets and prepares the project for upcoming features and improvements.

refactor: Enhance tool lifecycle management in AppState by implementing status tracking for tool messages. Introduce handling for secondary finish events to prevent redundant notifications and improve user experience. Update UI components to reflect tool status accurately, including new visual indicators for running, completed, and failed states.

refactor: Update Drawer components to enhance visual consistency and improve shadow effects. Replace background colors and adjust border opacity for better integration with the design system. Refine MessageBubble and DrawerView styles to align with the overall UI aesthetics.

refactor: Update CommandBar components for improved consistency and visual alignment. Adjust CommandBarWindowController to match SwiftUI CommandBarView width, enhance color usage in CommandBar components, and refine layout in QuickConfirmView and DrawerView for better user experience. Optimize text field styles and shadows across various UI elements.

chore: Update .gitignore to include new directories and files for build artifacts and development tools. Add 'dist/', '.cursor/', '.sourcekit-lsp/', and 'buildServer.json' to streamline project management and prevent unnecessary file tracking.

refactor: Enhance error handling in MotiveApp by introducing a graceful exit dialog for database initialization failures. Update AppDelegate to replace timer-based permission checks with task-based management for improved concurrency. Refactor various components to utilize Task for delayed actions, ensuring smoother UI interactions and responsiveness.

feat: Add currentToolInput property to AppState for better tool input management. Update CommandBar views to utilize currentToolInput directly for displaying tool input details, enhancing user experience during tool execution.

refactor: Improve data storage management by creating a dedicated directory for SwiftData in Application Support. Update database deletion logic to reference the new store URL. Enhance OpenCodeBridge to handle session.idle events in non-JSON log lines, ensuring proper event handling. Refactor CommandBar views to streamline status display with improved text handling and truncation.

refactor: Simplify CommandBar visibility management by introducing isCommandBarVisible property in AppState. Update toggleCommandBar method in AppDelegate to utilize this new property. Remove unnecessary pre-warming of CommandBar window to prevent visual glitches. Enhance CommandBarWindowController to handle fade-in and fade-out animations for improved user experience. Streamline CommandBarView by removing redundant entrance animation logic.

refactor: Update AppDelegate to show Command Bar on dock icon click instead of Settings window. Enhance AppState to reset menu bar state on error and ensure workspace directory exists on startup. Introduce WorkspaceManager for managing workspace operations and bootstrap files. Add IdentityParser for parsing agent identity from IDENTITY.md. Implement PersonaSettingsView for editing agent persona and workspace files, including identity, soul, and user information. Update localization for new persona settings and templates.
chore: Add changelog configuration to release.yml for categorizing GitHub release notes by features, bug fixes, maintenance, updates, and security.
chore: Add release.yml configuration file to manage GitHub releases and streamline deployment processes.
fix: Adjust CompactTabButton in SettingsView to ensure consistent icon sizing by setting a fixed frame of 24x24 for improved layout and visual alignment.

chore: Remove deprecated skills including Discord, Sherpa ONNX TTS, Slack, Trello, and Voice Call from the Skills bundle to streamline functionality and reduce maintenance overhead. Update SkillsSettingsView to display missing configuration details for improved user guidance.

releases.name: v0.11.4
releases.body:
## 0.11.4

### ✨ New Features

**Multi-line Input**
Command bar and drawer chat input now support multi-line editing. Use `Shift+Return` to insert a new line, `Return` to submit. The input grows up to 6 lines and shows a scrollbar when content overflows.

**Live Plan Preview**
When the agent writes a plan file during a session, a preview window now opens automatically with live markdown rendering — letting you follow along as the plan takes shape.

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

**SSE Streaming Fixes**
- Fixed prompt echo: user messages were occasionally being rendered as assistant bubbles
- Improved `message.part.updated` snapshot tracking to correctly emit only incremental deltas, preventing duplicate text in the conversation view

**Input Scroll Behavior**
- Fixed text cursor and placeholder misaligned (too low) in command bar and drawer input
- Fixed scroll position not resetting to top after clearing multi-line content
- Fixed scrollbar staying at top after paste — now scrolls to cursor so the last line is always fully visible

**Status Bar**
- Improved status bar update reliability during running sessions

### 🔧 Improvements

- Localization strings added for glass effect and window position settings (en / ja / zh-Hans)
- QuickConfirm: improved custom input handling and button state transitions
- Deprecated provider icon assets removed (Groq, MiniMax, Mistral, Qwen, Bedrock, Cohere, DeepInfra, Moonshot, etc.)

### 🧪 Tests

- Added `SSEClientTests` covering streaming, prompt echo filtering, and delta tracking
- Added `ConfigManagerProviderTests`


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/geezerrrr/motive/compare/v0.11.3...v0.11.4

releases.name: v0.11.2
releases.body:
## v0.11.2

### Bug Fixes
- **LM Studio**: Fix config handling when API key is empty #33 

### Improvements
- **Hotkeys**: Use Carbon APIs for more reliable global shortcuts #32 
- **UI**: Updates and refinements to Command Bar, layout, and visuals #32 
- **Assets**: Remove redundant SVG assets (Groq, MiniMax, Mistral, Qwen, bedrock, cohere, deepinfra, moonshot) in favor of consolidated icon set

### Maintenance
- SwiftFormat and SwiftLint updates


## New Contributors
* @git-napkin made their first contribution in https://github.com/geezerrrr/motive/pull/32

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/geezerrrr/motive/compare/v0.11.1...v0.11.2

releases.name: v0.8.1
releases.body:
## v0.8.1

### Bug Fixes

- **Skill runtime PATH mismatch**: Fixed skills showing "Ready" but failing to find binaries (e.g., `grizzly`) at runtime. Unified PATH construction between `CommandRunner.effectivePaths()` and OpenCode's runtime environment.
- **Skill toggle kills running tasks**: Removed auto-restart on skill toggle. Agent now restarts immediately when idle, or defers until the current task completes.
- **Disabled skills leaked into AI prompt**: System prompt now only includes explicitly enabled skills, preventing the AI from calling permission-denied tools.
- **Stale state after data deletion**: Added detection for when `~/.motive/` is deleted but UserDefaults persists, auto-resetting project paths and token usage data.

### Improvements

- **Unified shimmer animation**: Drawer thinking indicator now shares the same metallic shimmer effect as the menu bar.
- **UI color consistency**: Replaced hardcoded colors with system semantic colors across CommandBar and DesignSystem.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/geezerrrr/motive/compare/v0.8.0...v0.8.1

releases.name: v0.11.0
releases.body:
## v0.8.0

### ✨ What's New
- Added **scheduled task management**: create, configure, and execute scheduled tasks from Settings.
- Improved **UI consistency** across core surfaces (Command Bar, Drawer, Quick Confirm, Settings, Status Notification).
- Refined mode color behavior and spacing/readability details for a cleaner experience.

### 🐛 Bug Fixes
- Fixed a critical issue where **OpenRouter configured models** were not applied correctly.
- Slash-based model IDs (e.g. `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4`) are now preserved and routed properly.

### ✅ Quality
- Added/updated tests for scheduler and model payload routing behavior.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/geezerrrr/motive/compare/v0.10.1...v0.11.0

readme_html:
Why Motive?

AI coding agents are powerful, but they all assume you're watching. Switch away from the terminal or editor, and you'll come back to find the agent stuck on a permission approval that's been sitting there for minutes.

Motive puts the agent in your menu bar. No window to babysit. When it needs a yes/no or has a question, a native popup drops down — no matter what app you're in. You respond, it continues, you go back to what you were doing.

Under the hood it uses OpenCode as the agent engine. Motive doesn't try to be a better agent — it just makes sure the agent can reach you.

Desktop Apps CLI Tools Motive
Where it lives App window Terminal Menu bar
When it needs you Buried in UI Waits in terminal Native popup
Switch away? Miss responses Miss prompts Finds you

Demo

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6209e3d9-60db-4166-a14a-ae90cdbc01d6

Features

Core

  • Background execution — The agent runs in the background. No window to watch, no terminal to babysit.
  • Native popups — Permission requests and questions appear as macOS-native popups from the menu bar.
  • Ambient status — Menu bar icon shows progress at a glance without demanding attention.
  • Concurrent sessions — Run multiple tasks in parallel, each working independently.

Control & Privacy

  • Trust levels — Three modes to control what the agent can do on its own:

    Level Behavior
    Careful Asks before every edit and shell command
    Balanced Auto-approves safe actions, asks for unknown commands
    Yolo Full autonomy for trusted environments

  • Approval system — Fine-grained file permission policies with per-action Always Allow / Ask / Deny.

  • Local-first — All data stays on your machine. Only API requests leave your device.

Extensibility

  • Multiple providers — Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, Ollama, OpenRouter, Azure, Bedrock, and more. Bring your own key.
  • 50+ built-in skills — GitHub, Slack, Notion, Calendar, and others. Enable/disable in Settings.
  • Custom skills — Create your own in ~/.motive/skills/, no code changes required.
  • Browser automation — Web scraping, form filling, and multi-step browser workflows.

Built for Mac

  • Native macOS — Swift 6, SwiftUI, AppKit. No Electron, no web views.
  • Keychain storage — API keys stored securely in macOS Keychain.
  • Global hotkey — ⌥Space from anywhere, like Spotlight.
  • Multi-language UI — English, 简体中文, 日本語.

Quick Start

Install

Chip Download
Apple Silicon Motive-arm64.dmg
Intel Motive-x86_64.dmg

First launch: macOS may block unsigned apps. Go to System Settings → Privacy & Security → Click "Open Anyway".

Configure

  1. Click the menu bar icon → Settings
  2. Select your AI provider (Claude / OpenAI / Gemini / Ollama)
  3. Enter your API key

Use

  1. Press ⌥Space to open the command bar
  2. Describe what you want done, press Enter
  3. The command bar disappears — the agent works in the background
  4. When the agent needs approval or has a question, a native popup appears
  5. Check the menu bar icon anytime for status; click to view details

For detailed guides, see the documentation.

Keyboard Shortcuts

Shortcut Action
⌥Space Open command bar
↵ Submit task
Esc Dismiss command bar
⌘, Open settings

FAQ

Cursor needs you in its window. Claude Code needs you in the terminal. Switch away and you won't notice when they need input.

Motive runs the agent as a background process. When it needs input, a native popup appears from the menu bar regardless of what app you're in.

Anything OpenCode can do — refactor code, generate files, run scripts, organize projects, write docs, and more. OpenCode handles the agent work; Motive handles the macOS experience on top.

No. Sessions and history stay on your machine. The only network traffic is API requests to your chosen AI provider. Use Ollama for fully offline operation.

Yes. Select Ollama as your provider and point it to your local instance.

To register the global hotkey (⌥Space) that opens the command bar from anywhere.

Roadmap

  • Scheduled tasks — recurring or time-based tasks that run automatically
  • iOS companion — send tasks to your Mac from your iPhone
  • Multi-agent workflows — orchestrate multiple agents on related tasks

Build from Source

git clone https://github.com/geezerrrr/motive.git
cd motive
open Motive.xcodeproj

The OpenCode binary is bundled automatically during release builds. For development, place it at Motive/Resources/opencode.

Requirements

  • macOS 15.0 (Sequoia) or later
  • API key for Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, or local Ollama setup
